role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Correctly identified the absence of visible text and remained within the linguistic domain by noting the lack of script evidence.
role_adherence_notes.landscape: Stayed within terrain and climate analysis, correctly identifying the high-altitude plateau and basaltic soil.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Focused on vegetation types (montane grasslands, shrubs) and biome distribution without drifting into architecture.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Analyzed road infrastructure (asphalt quality, lack of signage) and construction materials, which fits the regulatory/infrastructure scope.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized cross-domain cues (architecture + camera generation + terrain) to distinguish between geographically similar regions.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The pipeline correctly identified Lesotho. The specialists provided high-quality, image-grounded evidence (rondavels, high-altitude grasslands) that effectively distinguished the location from other highland regions. The tournament logic correctly prioritized the specific combination of architectural and landscape features found in Lesotho over the broader South African analog.

visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Descriptions of rolling grassy mountains and basaltic soil match the image perfectly.
visual_consistency_notes.botanics: Descriptions of montane grasslands and sparse shrubs are consistent with the visual evidence.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Observations about the road quality and lack of bollards are accurate.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: Descriptions of the rondavels and concrete block structures match the image.
